name: Me of Enki
description: The Me of Enki are a collection of divine decrees, fundamental rules, social institutions, religious practices, technologies, arts, crafts, and all aspects of civilization, culture, and existence, believed to be held by the god Enki, the god of wisdom, intelligence, crafts, mischief, water, and creation. These Me are not physical objects, but rather abstract concepts or blueprints for how things should be in the world. They represent the very essence of civilization and order. Enki, in some myths, generously bestows these Me upon humanity, allowing them to develop and flourish. However, in the myth of Inanna and Enki, Inanna cunningly steals the Me from Enki, transferring them from his city of Eridu to her city of Uruk, thus granting Uruk power and prominence. The loss of the Me would have devastating consequences for any city or civilization. Their control signifies power, influence, and the ability to shape the world. The Me encompass everything from kingship and priesthood to writing, music, and metalworking, effectively defining the structure and function of Sumerian society. The number of Me is often cited as being around one hundred, although the exact list varies depending on the source. They are the foundation of Sumerian culture and the basis of their advanced civilization, representing the divine order established by the gods.
subtitle: Divine decrees shaping Sumerian civilization
origin: Created by the gods, held by Enki
currentLocation: Abstract; potentially dispersed or held in Uruk after Inanna's theft.

keyMyths.title: Inanna and Enki
keyMyths.description: Inanna travels to Eridu and, through trickery or persuasion, obtains the Me from Enki. She then brings them back to Uruk, granting her city significant power and influence.

keyMyths.title: Enki's Wisdom
keyMyths.description: Enki, as the god of wisdom, inherently possesses and understands the Me. He uses this knowledge to guide humanity and establish the foundations of civilization.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.description: A foundational text on Sumerian myths and legends.
sources.text: A foundational text on Sumerian myths and legends.

sources.author: Thorkild Jacobsen
